description: Provider-backed campaign that compares Kubernetes default scheduling with a load-aware custom scheduler for distributed LocalAI worker-mode workloads under L0 resource-aware conditions.

cycleGovernance.governancePolicy: compare default and load-aware scheduling under paired scenarios where workload, infrastructure, model mix, labels, monitoring and rescheduling policy are held constant.

scope.primaryQuestion: Does a load-aware custom scheduler using runtime CPU and memory annotations produce more balanced or better-performing LocalAI worker-mode placements than the Kubernetes default scheduler under paired L0 scenarios?

stateModel.profileStatus: Configuration-profile lifecycle state. This is the canonical meaning of the backward-compatible status field.
stateModel.configurationState: Whether the cycle configuration is planned, active, locked, superseded, or deprecated.
stateModel.executionState: Latest known execution outcome for the cycle or campaign.
stateModel.status: Backward-compatible alias of profileStatus; execution outcomes must be read from executionState.

infrastructureSummarySemantics.description: The cycle is expected to evaluate several worker-node counts through variant-scoped infrastructure profiles. Scenario-level evidence must identify the concrete infrastructure profile used for each run.
